rationale: The cited paper is used as part of the methodological foundation when the authors introduce and justify their HB two-stage consider-then-choose model. In the citing paper, Aribarg et al. (2014) appears in the key sections that motivate modeling of screening plus final choice, which is central to Studies 2 and 3. Although it is cited in a group and not for its private-label substantive findings, its role is more than a passing background mention because it supports the analytical approach actually implemented.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Aribarg et al. (2014) as prior work on two-stage screening-then-choice modeling and applies that modeling approach to conjoint tasks about scale discriminability. The cited work functions as methodological precedent for the paper's screening/choice estimation setup.
